verses.text: David calls on 'the God of my righteousness,' who enlarged him in distress, and asks how long men will love vanity and turn his glory to shame. He appeals to the God who has helped before. Past mercies give boldness to pray in present trouble.

David calls on 'the God of my righteousness,' who enlarged him in distress, and asks how long men will love vanity and turn his glory to shame. He appeals to the God who has helped before. Past mercies give boldness to pray in present trouble.

The LORD has set apart the godly for Himself and hears them; so, 'stand in awe, and sin not,' commune with your heart, and offer righteous sacrifices in trust. Reverence and self-examination steady the soul. To know we are God's own is the cure for restless anger.

The LORD has set apart the godly for Himself and hears them; so, 'stand in awe, and sin not,' commune with your heart, and offer righteous sacrifices in trust. Reverence and self-examination steady the soul. To know we are God's own is the cure for restless anger.

The LORD has set apart the godly for Himself and hears them; so, 'stand in awe, and sin not,' commune with your heart, and offer righteous sacrifices in trust. Reverence and self-examination steady the soul. To know we are God's own is the cure for restless anger.

When many ask, 'who will shew us any good?' David prays, 'Lord, lift thou up the light of thy countenance,' and finds a gladness greater than harvest joy, lying down to sleep in peace. God's favour outshines all earthly plenty. His smile gives a rest the world cannot give.
